revision.text: #redirect[[en:Ozaho_no_Ōkimi]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''袁邪本王'''(おざほのみこ;生没年不詳)は[[日本神話]]に登場する[[皇族]]。[[彦坐王]]第二王子で、[[開化天皇]]の孫に当たる。[[葛野別]]・[[近淡海蚊野別]]の祖。母は春日建国勝戸売(戸売とあることから女性であることが分かるが、父は明らかではない。)の娘沙本之大闇見戸売（さほのおおくらみとめ）。同母の兄弟に[[日下部連]]・[[甲斐国造]]の祖[[狭穂彦王]]、[[若狭耳別]]の祖[[室毘古王]]と[[垂仁天皇]]皇后[[狭穂姫命]]がいる。
王は父の彦坐王と共に[[滋賀県]][[愛知郡]][[秦荘町]]・[[軽野神社]]の祭神となっている他、[[佐波加刀神社]]の祭神に名を連ねている。
{{history-stub}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族|おさほのおおきみ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物|おさほのおおきみ]]

{{history-stub}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族|おさほのおおきみ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物|おさほのおおきみ]]

'''袁邪本王'''(おざほのみこ;生没年不詳)は[[日本神話]]に登場する[[皇族]]。[[彦坐王]]第二王子で、[[開化天皇]]の孫に当たる。[[葛野別]]・[[近淡海蚊野別]]の祖。母は春日建国勝戸売(戸売とあることから女性であることが分かるが、父は明らかではない。)の娘沙本之大闇見戸売（さほのおおくらみとめ）。同母の兄弟に[[日下部連]]・[[甲斐国造]]の祖[[狭穂彦王]]、[[若狭耳別]]の祖[[室毘古王]]と[[垂仁天皇]]皇后[[狭穂姫命]]がいる。

revision.text:
'''袁邪本王'''（おざほのみこ、生没年不詳）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]。
== 概要 == 袁邪本王は父の彦坐王と共に[[滋賀県]][[愛知郡 (滋賀県)|愛知郡]][[秦荘町]]・[[軽野神社]]の祭神となっている他、滋賀県[[長浜市]]・[[佐波加刀神社]]の祭神に名を連ねている。
なお兄の[[狭穂彦王]]は[[甲斐国造]]の祖とされるが、[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に活動した初代国造の[[塩海足尼]]が[[垂仁天皇|垂仁朝]]に活動した狭穂彦王の3世孫とするのは世代的に無理があり、塩海足尼の父とされる臣知津彦命の別名が袁邪本王である可能性が考えられる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm 甲斐国造の系譜と一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
== 系譜 == 父は[[開化天皇]]の子である[[彦坐王]]、母は[[春日建国勝戸売]]の娘[[沙本之大闇見戸売]]（さほのおおくらみとめ）で、[[葛野別]]・[[近淡海蚊野別]]の祖にあたる。同母の兄弟に[[日下部連]]・[[甲斐国造]]の祖・[[狭穂彦王]]や[[若狭耳別]]の祖・[[室毘古王]]、[[垂仁天皇]]皇后の[[狭穂姫命]]がいる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おさほのおおきみ}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:飛鳥時代以前の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]]

=== 宝賀寿男による異説 === 宝賀寿男は、兄の[[狭穂彦王]]は[[甲斐国造]]の祖とされるが、[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に活動した初代国造の[[塩海足尼]]が[[垂仁天皇|垂仁朝]]に活動した狭穂彦王の3世孫とするのは世代的に無理があり、塩海足尼の父とされる臣知津彦命の別名が袁邪本王であると主張している<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm 甲斐国造の系譜と一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。

revision.text:
'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:Figures of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Male members of the Imperial Family before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Unknown birth and death dates]]
[[ja:袁邪本王]]

revision.text:
'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==

His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.

Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>

revision.text:
'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.

According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.

revision.text:
'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}

'''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族|12=draft}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Aichigun, Shiga|ja|愛知郡 (滋賀県)|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagahama|ja|長浜市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=draft}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=draft}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命|12=draft}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=draft}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売|12=draft}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別|12=draft}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王|12=draft}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sahohime no Mikoto|ja|狭穂姫命|12=draft}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}}

11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:袁邪本王 up to rev 88398442
revision.text:
'''袁邪本王'''（おざほのみこ、生没年不詳）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[皇族]]。
== 概要 == 袁邪本王は父の彦坐王と共に[[滋賀県]][[愛知郡 (滋賀県)|愛知郡]][[秦荘町]]・[[軽野神社]]の祭神となっている他、滋賀県[[長浜市]]・[[佐波加刀神社]]の祭神に名を連ねている。
=== 宝賀寿男による異説 === 宝賀寿男は、兄の[[狭穂彦王]]は[[甲斐国造]]の祖とされるが、[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に活動した初代国造の[[塩海足尼]]が[[垂仁天皇|垂仁朝]]に活動した狭穂彦王の3世孫とするのは世代的に無理があり、塩海足尼の父とされる臣知津彦命の別名が袁邪本王であると主張している<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm 甲斐国造の系譜と一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
== 系譜 == 父は[[開化天皇]]の子である[[彦坐王]]、母は[[春日建国勝戸売]]の娘[[沙本之大闇見戸売]]（さほのおおくらみとめ）で、[[葛野別]]・[[近淡海蚊野別]]の祖にあたる。同母の兄弟に[[日下部連]]・[[甲斐国造]]の祖・[[狭穂彦王]]や[[若狭耳別]]の祖・[[室毘古王]]、[[垂仁天皇]]皇后の[[狭穂姫命]]がいる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おさほのおおきみ}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

== 系譜 == 父は[[開化天皇]]の子である[[彦坐王]]、母は[[春日建国勝戸売]]の娘[[沙本之大闇見戸売]]（さほのおおくらみとめ）で、[[葛野別]]・[[近淡海蚊野別]]の祖にあたる。同母の兄弟に[[日下部連]]・[[甲斐国造]]の祖・[[狭穂彦王]]や[[若狭耳別]]の祖・[[室毘古王]]、[[垂仁天皇]]皇后の[[狭穂姫命]]がいる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おさほのおおきみ}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|qid=Q11487910}}|mother={{ill|Q13360104|qid=Q13360104}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|qid=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|qid=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Tanba no Michinushi|qid=Q11368587}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|qid=Q11466268}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11626514]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family|qq=|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} figure of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karuno Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echi District, Shiga|Aichigun, Shiga]], Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioumi Atane|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}, who served during the [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, a child of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkuramito-me|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno Betsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Ōkimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578852}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Mimine Betsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * [[:en:Saho-hime|Sahohime no Mikoto]], Empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of figures before the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|mother={{ill|Q13360104|WD=Q13360104}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Tanba no Michinushi|WD=Q11368587}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11626514]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} figure of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karuno Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echi District, Shiga|Aichigun, Shiga]], Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioumi Atane|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}, who served during the [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, a child of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkuramito-me|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578852}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * [[:en:Saho-hime|Sahohime no Mikoto]], Empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of figures before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|mother={{ill|Q13360104|WD=Q13360104}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Tanba no Michinushi|WD=Q11368587}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11626514]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} figure of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karuno Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echi District, Shiga|Aichigun, Shiga]], Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioumi Atane|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}, who served during the [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, a child of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkuramito-me|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578852}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * [[:en:Saho-hime|Sahohime no Mikoto]], Empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].

His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578852}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * [[:en:Saho-hime|Sahohime no Mikoto]], Empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|mother={{ill|Q13360104|WD=Q13360104}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Tanba no Michinushi|WD=Q11368587}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11626514]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:52, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} figure of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karuno Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echi District, Shiga|Aichigun, Shiga]], Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]], Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioumi Atane|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}, who served during the [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, a child of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]], and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkuramito-me|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578852}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * [[:en:Saho-hime|Sahohime no Mikoto]], Empress of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]].

According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioumi Atane|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}, who served during the [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910}}|mother={{ill|Q13360104|WD=Q13360104}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755}}; {{ill|Tanba no Michinushi|WD=Q11368587}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268}}}} '''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karuno Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Echi District, Shiga|en|Echi District, Shiga|WD=Q1131097|lt=Aichigun, Shiga}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioumi Atane|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkuramito-me|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578852}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Saho-hime|en|Saho-hime|WD=Q11571754|lt=Sahohime no Mikoto}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|List of figures before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of figures before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkuramito-me|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578852|1=Murobiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Saho-hime|en|Saho-hime|WD=Q11571754|lt=Sahohime no Mikoto|1=Saho-hime}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}.
