<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:袁邪本王]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|father={{ill|Hikoimasu|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}|mother={{ill|Q13360104|WD=Q13360104|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me}}|relatives={{ill|Empress Sahohime|WD=Q11571754|1=Saho-hime}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|WD=Q11571755|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Tanba no Michinushi|WD=Q11368587|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|WD=Q11466268|1=Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka}}}} '''Ozaho no Ōkimi''' (袁邪本王, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} figure of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.
== Overview ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi is enshrined alongside his father, {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, as a deity at the {{ill|Karuno Shrine|ja|軽野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karuno Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Echi District, Shiga|en|Echi District, Shiga|WD=Q1131097|lt=Aichigun, Shiga|1=Echi District, Shiga}}, Shiga Prefecture. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Sawaka Shrine|ja|佐波加刀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, Shiga Prefecture.
=== Alternative Theory by Tsutomu Hōga ===
According to historian Tsutomu Hōga, while Ozaho's brother {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}} is traditionally considered the ancestor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, the generational gap between Sahohiko no Ōkimi (active during the {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} era) and the first Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Shioumi Atane|ja|塩海足尼|1=Shiomi no Sukune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioumi Atane|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiomi no Sukune|qq=}}, who served during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} era, makes this connection problematic. Hōga proposes that Ozaho no Ōkimi was another name for {{ill|Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|臣知津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomochitsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, identified as the father of Shioumi Atane.<ref>Tsutomu Hōga, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm Genealogy of Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko]," ''Kojiki-no-Heika Room'', 2016.</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ozaho no Ōkimi was the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|qq=}}, a child of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|WD=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}, and his consort, {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkuramito-me|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja|春日建国勝戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Takekunikatsutome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He is considered an ancestor of the {{ill|Katsuno Betsu|ja|葛野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja|近淡海蚊野別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsuomi Kanobetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineages. His siblings include: * {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. * {{ill|Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja|室毘古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Ōkimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578852|1=Murobiko no Mikoto}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja|若狭耳別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasa Mimine Betsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Saho-hime|en|Saho-hime|WD=Q11571754|lt=Sahohime no Mikoto|1=Saho-hime}}, Empress of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}.
